_meta.warning: ALL PATIENTS BELOW ARE FICTIONAL AND SYNTHETIC. No real patient data is used, ingested, or represented. Any resemblance to a real person is coincidental. This is a non-validated humanitarian proof-of-concept.
_meta.step: Step 2 of 10 — data artifacts only (no risk math, no agent logic).
_meta.step_4_5_rebalance_note: Step 4.5: with the real EuroSCORE II calculator (Step 3), SYNTH-007/009/010/011/013 were rebalanced by adding FIXED-factor burden (poor LV, extracardiac arteriopathy, moderate renal, recent MI, and/or moderate pulmonary hypertension) so each baseline lands in the declined band (>=6% OPERABILITY_THRESHOLD) while its euroscore_visible levers still bring the optimized score below threshold. (SYNTH-010, baseline 5.56%, was a fifth misfit not in the original Step-4.5 target list but failing the same property; fixed the same way.) Their modifiable_levers arrays and the euroscore_visible fields those levers map to were NOT changed. SYNTH-008 was reclassified from reversible_with_optimization to fixed_high_risk: its visible-lever optimization helps materially (22.4% -> 7.45%) but cannot cross the threshold. Category counts are now: operable_at_baseline 5, reversible_with_optimization 7, fixed_high_risk 6. Enforced by data/validate.py's score-property check.
_meta.coupling_design_note: Each modifiable lever a vignette carries is tagged 'euroscore_visible' (improving it changes a EuroSCORE II input directly) or 'needs_risk_modifier' (EuroSCORE II cannot see it directly; a supplementary modifier layer is deferred to Step 3). CONSTRAINT: every 'reversible_with_optimization' vignette carries at least two 'euroscore_visible' modifiable levers in a burdened state, so the reversal loop can demonstrably move the EuroSCORE II score BEFORE any supplementary modifier exists. NOTE / AMBIGUITY: SPEC.md section f names six modifiable levers (glycemic control, pulmonary/asthma, smoking, anemia, nutrition/albumin, blood pressure), of which only pulmonary/asthma maps to a EuroSCORE II input (chronic_lung_disease). To satisfy the coupling requirement this step ALSO treats mobility (poor_mobility), heart-failure symptoms (nyha_class), and critical pre-op state (critical_preoperative_state) as modifiable euroscore_visible levers, as directed by the Step 2 task. SPEC.md section d/f currently describes NYHA/cardiac factors as 'largely fixed'; this extension must be reconciled with SPEC.md. [TO VERIFY — reconcile modifiable-lever set between SPEC.md section f and Step 2 vignettes].

vignettes.rationale: Younger, well-preserved heart, no lung disease, no meaningful modifiable burden. Predicted risk should already sit below any reasonable operability threshold; included as a control case that needs no reversal.

clinical_context.coronary_disease_extent: Two-vessel disease, stable angina, CABG elected over PCI (synthetic).

modifiable_levers.note: Mildly elevated BP; not a EuroSCORE II input. [TO VERIFY — BP target per SPEC.md section f].

vignettes.rationale: Low fixed burden, good ventricular function, no diabetes. Only a single soft modifiable factor; well within operable range at baseline.

modifiable_levers.note: Already in cessation; not a EuroSCORE II input. [TO VERIFY — recommended cessation interval per SPEC.md section f].

vignettes.rationale: Type 2 diabetic but diet/oral controlled (not on insulin) and good heart function. Baseline risk low; glycemic lever is minor and invisible to EuroSCORE II.

modifiable_levers.note: Oral-controlled diabetes, HbA1c slightly above target; not a EuroSCORE II input. [TO VERIFY — HbA1c target per SPEC.md section f].

vignettes.rationale: Minimal comorbidity, no modifiable levers of note. Pure control case: agent should recognize no reversal is required.
Moderate age with well-controlled profile and preserved LV function. One soft nutritional lever, no euroscore-visible burden; baseline operable.

modifiable_levers.note: Borderline nutrition; not a EuroSCORE II input. [TO VERIFY — albumin/nutrition target per SPEC.md section f].

vignettes.rationale: GRANDMOTHER ANALOG. Elderly woman from Sihor with ~90% coronary blockage (CABG indicated), type 2 diabetes on insulin, and poorly controlled asthma, declined as too risky. Her elevated risk is driven substantially by MODIFIABLE, EuroSCORE-visible burden: uncontrolled asthma (chronic_lung_disease), deconditioning (poor_mobility), and heart-failure symptoms (NYHA III), plus non-visible levers (HbA1c, anemia). Optimizing the visible levers should move the EuroSCORE II score toward operable even before the Step 3 modifier layer exists.

clinical_context.coronary_disease_extent: ~90% proximal LAD stenosis, CABG indicated (synthetic; mirrors the motivating case).

modifiable_levers.note: Frequent exacerbations; maps to EuroSCORE II chronic_lung_disease input. [TO VERIFY — asthma to chronic_lung_disease mapping per SPEC.md section d].
Deconditioning amenable to prehabilitation; maps to EuroSCORE II poor_mobility input.

vignettes.rationale: Declined patient whose risk is inflated by uncontrolled asthma and deconditioning (both euroscore-visible), on top of FIXED cardiac/vascular burden: poor left ventricular function, extracardiac arteriopathy, and a recent myocardial infarction. Rebalanced in Step 4.5 against the real EuroSCORE II model so the baseline sits in the declined band (>=6%) while optimizing the visible levers still crosses below threshold.

vignettes.rationale: HONESTY CASE (optimization-insufficient variant). Substantial modifiable burden (critical preoperative state + poorly controlled asthma), and visible-lever optimization moves the risk a lot (22.4% -> 7.45%), but even fully optimized the patient stays above OPERABILITY_THRESHOLD. The agent must do real optimization work AND still correctly decline. Distinguished from the no-lever fixed_high_risk cases: here optimization genuinely helps but cannot cross the threshold.

vignettes.rationale: HONESTY CASE. Very elderly, redo surgery, poor LV, recent MI, severe renal impairment and vascular disease. Risk is dominated by FIXED factors; the only modifiable lever is already optimized, so no reversal pathway should bring this below threshold. The agent must be able to say 'no'.

modifiable_levers.note: BP already at target; not a EuroSCORE II input and no headroom to reduce risk. [TO VERIFY — BP target per SPEC.md section f].
